title: State Police searching for Berkshire County man after suspicious early-morning fire
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Police suspect Monterey fire was intentionally set, search ongoing for homeowner John Rohane, 60.
Fire reported at 3:50 a.m. at 340 Main Road, no injuries occurred.
Public advised to call 911 if they see Rohane, possibly in green jacket and boots.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Tessa, an 11-year-old yellow Labrador, was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth using pepperoni as bait.
Falmouth Fire and Rescue identified the sinkhole was caused by a leak in an underground pipe system.
Rescuers used a pole leash to safely extract the uninjured dog after two hours, preventing potential ground collapse risks.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue takes in 16 axolotls with injuries and infections from an overcrowding situation.
The rescue is seeking donations for veterinary care and long-term support of the rehabilitating axolotls.
Founded in 2024, the nonprofit focuses on axolotl education, rehabilitation, and adoption throughout New England.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters discusses how imperfections symbolize friendship, partnership, and growth.
A24 offers Love Letters tickets for a Boston film preview with Zendaya and Robert Pattinson.
Yowei Shaw shares how a burn mark on a table became a symbol of relationship growth.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School has opened a 25-station student-run salon for cosmetology training.
The salon allows students to gain hands-on experience with public clients and works towards professional licensure.
Students can accumulate 1,000 training hours to qualify for the state licensing exam.

title: CeCarré brings a $48 gold-and-caviar-topped pizza to the Pru
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: CeCarré Pizza & Provisions opened at the Prudential Center on March 7, offering a $48 gold and caviar pizza.
The Roman-style pizzeria serves a variety of pies, focaccia sandwiches, salads, arancini, and desserts.
Owner Nina Patel previously ran Descendant Pizza, now rebranded as CeCarré at the Prudential Center.

title: Mass. woman killed in 6-vehicle crash in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 69-year-old Massachusetts woman died in a six-vehicle crash on I-75 in Ochopee, Florida, involving a semi-truck.
The crash occurred when a truck failed to slow, causing a collision starting with an SUV driven by a 71-year-old man.
Two people, including the SUV driver, were seriously injured and hospitalized; the incident is under investigation by Florida Highway Patrol.

title: Former Miss Hall’s School teacher at center of grooming allegations is charged with rape
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matthew Rutledge, former Miss Hall's School teacher, charged with three counts of rape for alleged assaults on students.
Previously unprosecutable allegations against Rutledge date back to 1990s-2010 and involve grooming, sexual advances, and assault.
Rutledge's arraignment scheduled for April 22 as investigation continues; former students Fares and Simon testified to grand jury.

title: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: FIFA is reducing hotel room bookings in Boston for the World Cup, impacting local tourism projections.
Seven World Cup matches will take place at the temporarily renamed Boston Stadium, formerly Gillette Stadium.
Meet Boston plans to market released rooms to transient visitors, anticipating high demand from ticket sales.

title: ‘I had just lost everything’: Kelsey Fitzsimmons testifies in her trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, testified about being shot by Officer Patrick Noonan during a restraining order service.
Fitzsimmons discussed her personal and professional history, describing her emotional state during the events leading to the shooting.
Closing arguments in Fitzsimmons's trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon are scheduled for Thursday morning.
